q: What ages are LernZeit flashcard games designed for?
a: Our collection is designed for children aged 4–10. Each game has its own recommended age and skill level, so you can choose what best suits your child’s current stage.

q: How do I choose the right game or level for my child?
a: Start with the skill you would like to support—such as storytelling, asking questions, vocabulary or English tenses. Choose Level 1 for beginners, Level 2 for children ready for more challenge, or use our product picker for a personalised recommendation.

q: How much time and preparation do these games need?
a: Most activities can be played in just 5–10 minutes. The games are ready to use, with simple prompts and guidance built into the cards—no worksheets or lesson planning required.

q: Can children use the cards independently?
a: Many children can sort, arrange, observe and flip the cards on their own. Younger children may benefit from playing with a parent, while older children can gradually explore more independently.

q: How are LernZeit games different from regular flashcards?
a: They go beyond memorising facts. Children observe, think, speak, arrange, question and solve—then flip the card to check or continue the activity. Each set also includes multiple ways to play.

q: Are the cards made for repeated use?
a: Yes. The cards have double-sided lamination and child-safe rounded corners, making them suitable for regular use at home, during travel or in learning spaces.

q: Can LernZeit games be used in classrooms or small groups?
a: Yes. Educators can use them for warm-ups, learning centres, guided discussions, group activities or individual practice. The open-ended formats allow activities to be adapted for different learners.

q: Can language-development therapists use these cards in sessions?
a: The cards can be used as supplementary prompts for vocabulary, sequencing, question formation, storytelling and expressive language. They are educational resources—not diagnostic or therapeutic tools—and should be used according to the professional’s judgement.
